pages.raw_text:
be a float emblematic Of that line
of work. There will also be q float
suggestive of the cause of equal
suffrage. d. c. Coates has been
chosen as marshal of the day and
is now at work on the general de-
tails Of the parade.


The parade will form at River.
side and Monroe at $3.50 a. m. and
at 10 sharp will move east on Riv-
erside to Bernard, south on Ber-
nard to First, west on First to
Lincoln, where the parade will
countermarch east on First so that
the marchers will be given an op-
portunity to see the procession.
The parade will disband near the
corner of First and Bernard.


The speaking and athletic pro-
gram will be held at Natatorium
park in the afternoon.
